Class IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.isabelletranslation.gui.controller.IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator
All Implemented Interfaces:
IsabelleLauncherListener
public class IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator extends Object implements IsabelleLauncherListener
Updates the IsabelleProgressDialog for a given IsabelleLauncher.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator(Proof proof, IsabelleLauncher launcher)
    Creates a new mediator for the given proof and launcher

    • IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator

      public IsabelleLauncherProgressDialogMediator(Proof proof, IsabelleLauncher launcher)
      Creates a new mediator for the given proof and launcher
      Parameters:
      proof - the given proof
      launcher - the launcher used

  • Method Details

    • launcherStopped

      public void launcherStopped(IsabelleLauncher launcher, Collection<IsabelleSolver> finishedInstances)
      Description copied from interface: IsabelleLauncherListener
      Called when the launcher has stopped (both successfully and due to interruptions etc.).
      Specified by:
      launcherStopped in interface IsabelleLauncherListener
      Parameters:
      launcher - The stopped launcher
      finishedInstances - The instances which have finished processing in the launcher.

    • launcherStarted

      public void launcherStarted(IsabelleLauncher launcher, Collection<IsabelleSolver> solvers)
      Specified by:
      launcherStarted in interface IsabelleLauncherListener

    • launcherPreparationFinished

      public void launcherPreparationFinished(IsabelleLauncher launcher, Collection<IsabelleSolver> solvers)
      Specified by:
      launcherPreparationFinished in interface IsabelleLauncherListener

    • stopEvent

      protected void stopEvent()
      The event that occurs after the stop button has been pressed in the dialog. Uses IsabelleLauncher.stopAll() to interrupt the launcher. Also sets the userStopFlag so solver interrupts can be allocated to the user.

    • applyEvent

      protected void applyEvent()
      The event that occurs after the apply button has been pressed in the dialog. Invokes the applyResults() to close solved goals. Then disposes of the dialog.

    • discardEvent

      protected void discardEvent()
      The event that occurs after the discard button was pressed. Disposes of the dialog and does nothing else, as the button is only available once the launcher has stopped.
